read.primary_wall: The real contribution path is dominated by replaying q/grad_output for every K16 selected tile; same-shape attribution puts that tax near 9.76 ms.
read.secondary_wall: Reading/replaying the BF16 prob/scaled-dP page inside the contribution MFMA path adds about 4.14 ms.
read.not_primary_wall: Final compressed dKV atomics add about 1.48 ms on this branch, so optimizing only the final atomic stream cannot close the full sparse-attention gap.
read.selected_window_page_result: A wider selected-window P/dP page does improve q/grad_output reuse from K64 to K128, but best mode23 remains 17.28 ms before valid dKV atomics. K256 regresses to 26.28 ms, likely from LDS/page pressure. This family is not promotable.

next_kernel_contract.next_code_target: Implement a lighter producer-consumer handoff where the K16 score/prob/scaled-dP page is consumed by D-parallel contribution consumers before HBM, without staging a large selected-window page. The selected-window page family is rejected; the next surface needs either a persistent wave-level pipeline or a CK/FMHA-style block pipeline with q/grad_output resident across K tiles.
must_preserve: source-boundary inputs q, grad_output, output, lse, compressed KV, safe_groups, valid_u8
no materialized full [S, topk, H] probability or scaled-dP slab
S4096 latency gate measured before any e2e TFLOPs promotion
